title: calamintha menthifolia subsp. hirta
intro: Calamintha menthifolia subsp. hirta, also known as mint-leaved calamintha or mountain basil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Calamintha menthifolia subsp. hirta include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, relieve respiratory problems, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
